Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First, let me clearly understand the normative constraints: 1. Sequence: Application Received → Credit Check → Risk Assessment → Loan Decision → Inform Applicant → [If Approved: Finalize Documentation] → Close Case 2. Ordering Rules: - Credit Check before Risk Assessment. - Risk Assessment before Loan Decision. - Inform Applicant after Loan Decision. - If approved: Finalize Documentation before Close Case. - If rejected: do NOT finalize documentation before closing 3. Not Allowed: - Skipping activities in required sequence. - Changing order. - Finalize Documentation after rejection. - Closing case without following decision logic Desiderata: - Credit Check within 24 hours of Application Received - Inform Applicant within 48 hours of Loan Decision - Follow-up Call before closing case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: **Case 1:** - 09:00 Application Received - 09:30 Credit Check (30 min after app - within 24h) - 10:00 Risk Assessment (after credit check - OK) - 11:00 Loan Decision Approved (after risk assessment - OK) - 13:00 Inform Applicant (2 hours after decision - within 48h) - 13:30 Finalize Documentation (approved, so required - OK) - 14:00 Close Case (after finalize - OK) - No Follow-up Call mentioned. Normative: No violations. Sequence is perfect. Desiderata: Missing Follow-up Call

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: **Case 2:** - 08:00 Application Received - 08:20 Risk Assessment (NO prior Credit Check - violates normative ordering. Credit Check must occur before Risk Assessment) - 09:00 Credit Check (out of order) - 09:30 Loan Decision Rejected - 10:00 Finalize Documentation (Rejected loan - normative says do not finalize documentation before closing. This is a violation) - 10:30 Close Case - No Follow-up Call. Normative violations: 1. Order: Risk Assessment before Credit Check 2. Logic: Finalize Documentation after Rejection

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait, let me re-read the normative constraints. Inform Applicant must occur after Loan Decision. Yes: Following the loan decision, the applicant must be informed of the outcome. So skipping Inform Applicant is a violation. Let me verify Case 2 log carefully: - Application Received - Risk Assessment (08:20) - Credit Check (09:00) - Loan Decision (Rejected) (09:30) - Finalize Documentation (10:00) - Close Case (10:30) Missing: Inform Applicant. So normative violations in Case 2: - Risk Assessment before Credit Check (wrong order) - Missing Inform Applicant (skipped step) - Finalize Documentation after Rejection (contradictory action)

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: **Case 3:** - 2024-01-12 09:00 Application Received - 2024-01-14 10:00 Credit Check (more than 24h later - Jan 12 09:00 to Jan 14 10:00 is 49 hours. This violates desideratum, not normative) - 2024-01-14 11:00 Risk Assessment (after credit check - normative OK) - 2024-01-14 12:00 Loan Decision Approved - 2024-01-16 15:00 Inform Applicant (Jan 14 12:00 to Jan 16 15:00 is 51 hours. Desideratum says within 48h. Violates desideratum) - 2024-01-17 09:00 Finalize Documentation - 2024-01-17 10:00 Close Case - No Follow-up Call Normative for Case 3: No violations. Desiderata violations: - Credit Check not within 24h of application (49h delay) - Inform Applicant not within 48h of loan decision (51h delay) - No Follow-up Call

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me double-check if Case 2s Finalize Documentation is before Close Case technically yes, but since its a rejection, it shouldnt happen at all. Is there any issue with Case 2 missing Close Case without following logic? The log shows Close Case at 10:30. But because documentation was finalized, and the decision was rejected, closing the case after finalization violates the rule If loan is rejected, the case should be closed without finalizing documentation. Wait, I need to check if there are any other normative issues with Case 3. The sequence is: Application Received → Credit Check → Risk Assessment → Loan Decision → Inform Applicant → Finalize Documentation → Close Case. This matches exactly.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me calculate timestamps exactly: Case 3: - Application Received: 2024-01-12 09:00 (Friday) - Credit Check on Sunday 10am (49 hours later). - Loan Decision: 2024-01-14 12:00 (Sunday noon). Inform Applicant: 2024-01-16 15:00 (Tuesday 3pm). Thats 51 hours. Weekend in between. Jan 12 2024 was indeed a Friday. Jan 14 was Sunday. Jan 16 was Tuesday.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For Case 2 justifications: - Risk Assessment before Credit Check: Perhaps a preliminary assessment was done based on application data while waiting for external credit bureau response? Or manual override by a manager? System error. - Finalize Documentation after Rejection: Maybe the rejection was overturned or system logged incorrectly? Or Finalize Documentation here refers to rejection letter documentation? Agent prepared docs before decision updated. - Missing Inform Applicant: Applicant informed orally and not logged, or system failed to record. Case 3 interpretations: Weekend/holiday delays, reduced staffing. Applicant unreachable. Follow-up call deemed unnecessary. Case 1: Follow-up call skipped because case was straightforward and fast.
